signals.message: Deliver the seeneyu Design System for M1.

PM session complete (2026-03-22). Project status: M0–M8 all COMPLETE. App is live at https://seeneyu.vercel.app. Current phase: 7-marketing. M9 (Marketing Materials) is active — Marketer role is producing pitch deck + one-pager + outreach emails. BUG-001 fix assigned to Backend Engineer (ClipForm score ranges). No design tasks pending. FYI: BUG-002 (library page crash from missing 'use client' in ClipCard) was found and fixed. You may monitor ongoing work or stand by for the next phase.

New milestone M10: Script-Aware Coaching Loop. Design 3 UI components. Backend Engineer is working in parallel — deliver your specs to .shared/outputs/design/m10-spec.md ASAP so they can implement.

## Component 1 — CharacterBanner (record page, above webcam)
A compact horizontal banner that tells the learner exactly who they're mimicking.
Content: Character name (prominent), Actor name (secondary), Movie title (muted). Optional: a small icon or badge for the skill category.
Tone: Motivational, like a sports coaching card. 'You are mimicking Jordan Belfort (Leonardo DiCaprio) from The Wolf of Wall Street.'

## Component 2 — ScriptPanel (record page, below the observation checklist)
A card showing the exact dialogue or physical behavior the learner must perform.
If the clip has dialogue: show it as a blockquote — large readable text, styled like a script page.
If the clip has physical behavior (no dialogue): show a numbered instruction list.
Label: 'YOUR SCRIPT' or 'WHAT TO DO'.
Must be easy to read at a glance while the webcam is live.
Include a small tip like: 'Say this out loud while recording.'

## Component 3 — ActionPlan (feedback page, between improvements grid and AI Tips)
A numbered step list showing the learner's personalized improvement plan.
Each step card: step number (large, accent color), action text (bold, imperative), why text (small, muted, 1 line).
Title: 'YOUR ACTION PLAN'
Subtitle: 'Do these in order at your next practice session.'
Steps are ordered by priority (most impactful first).
Style: dark card with accent-left border per step number.

## Output
Write your specs to: .shared/outputs/design/m10-spec.md
Include ASCII mockup for each component.
Then write a signal to backend-engineer.json: 'M10 UI specs ready at .shared/outputs/design/m10-spec.md'.
Also signal pm.json: task-complete.

Three new milestones from user feedback. You have THREE design tasks — all can be started in parallel. Deliver each as a separate spec file.

---
## M11 — Observation Guide (file: .shared/outputs/design/m11-spec.md)
A structured pre-practice analysis shown on the clip detail page (before the learner clicks Record).
Layout: tabbed section below the YouTube embed. Tabs: 'Watch' (current) | 'How It Works' (new).
How It Works tab shows:
  - Headline: 'What [Character] does and why it works'
  - A vertical timeline of technique moments: timestamp badge | technique name | 1-line 'what' | 1-line 'why'
  - Example: [0:03] Direct Eye Contact — 'Holds gaze for 4 seconds without blinking' — 'Signals absolute confidence and makes the listener feel seen.'
  - Bottom: a 'Ready to practice?' CTA button → links to /record
Tone: like a sports coach pointing to film before a game. Clear, direct, educational.

---
## M12 — Micro-Practice Stepper (file: .shared/outputs/design/m12-spec.md)
Duolingo-style step-by-step practice flow. Replaces the current single record page with a multi-step flow at /library/[clipId]/practice/
Flow:
  - Step card (top): step number badge | skill focus label | instruction text | tip
  - Progress bar: horizontal step dots (1 completed/active/locked each)
  - Webcam recorder: same as current but with a 30-second max timer shown as a countdown ring
  - After each step: instant micro-feedback card slides in — pass/needs work. 'Next Step' or 'Retry' CTA.
  - After all steps complete: 'Full Performance' unlock screen — celebrate, then CTA to do full recording
Key constraint: each micro-step recording is max 30 seconds. Show countdown ring clearly.

---
## M13 — Onboarding Assessment & Learning Path (file: .shared/outputs/design/m13-spec.md)
Two parts:

Part A — Onboarding Assessment (shown after signup, before first visit to library):
  - 5-screen flow, one skill category per screen
  - Each screen: skill name + icon | brief description | self-rating slider (Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced) | optional: record a 10s sample clip
  - Progress bar across top: 1/5, 2/5...
  - Final screen: 'Assessing your level...' (AI scoring animation) → 'Your Learning Path Is Ready'

Part B — Learning Path Dashboard (at /dashboard or home page section):
  - 5 skill track columns (one per skill category)
  - Each column: current level badge | progress bar (clips completed / total for level) | next recommended clip card
  - LearningPathCard: clip thumbnail | skill badge | difficulty pill | 'Practice Now' CTA
  - SkillProgressBar: colored level track with milestone markers (Beginner → Intermediate → Advanced → Expert)

Output each spec separately. After all three are done, signal backend-engineer.json with a single message listing all three spec files. Also signal pm.json: task-complete.

SESSION END 2026-03-23. M10+M11+M12+M13 specs all delivered and implemented — great session. Next session: (1) Standby for any UI review requests from Tester on M11/M12/M13. (2) No new design tasks assigned yet. (3) Watch for: M13 dashboard layout may need review once real users interact with it — expect feedback on the 5-column grid on mobile. (4) Future milestone ideas: progress celebration animations, streak tracking UI, social sharing card for scores.

PM context window is at ~90% — session restart is imminent. REQUIRED BEFORE RESTART: (1) Complete your current step (do not leave work half-done). (2) Update your progress/checkpoint file in .shared/outputs/<your-role>/. (3) Write a "checkpoint-saved" signal to pm.json listing what you finished and what is next. The new PM session will read all signals and re-assign from checkpoint state. Your .shared/outputs/<role>/progress.json is the source of truth for recovery.

NEW MILESTONE M14: Library Expansion UI Design.

## CONTEXT
The library is expanding from 15 to 100+ clips. We also now track whether a clip has a publicly available screenplay (screenplaySource field). Users need to be able to browse and filter this larger library effectively.

## YOUR TASK
Design the following UI additions. Deliver to: .shared/outputs/design/m14-spec.md

### 1. Library Filter Enhancements (src/app/library/page.tsx)
Current filters: skill focus, difficulty level.
NEW filters to add:
- "Filter by Film" — searchable dropdown or multi-select, shows all unique movieTitles
- "Has Screenplay" — checkbox/toggle to show only screenplay-backed clips
- Keep existing skill + difficulty filters

Design the filter panel layout that accommodates all 4 filter types cleanly. For 100+ clips, consider whether pagination or infinite scroll is needed.

### 2. Screenplay Badge on ClipCard (src/components/ClipCard.tsx)
When a clip has a screenplaySource:
- Show a small badge (e.g., amber "📄 Screenplay" tag)
- Placement: bottom-left of card or below the skill badge
- Should be subtle but discoverable

### 3. Clip Detail Page — Screenplay Link
On src/app/library/[clipId]/page.tsx:
- When clip has screenplaySource, show a "Read Screenplay →" button
- Style: secondary/ghost button, opens in new tab
- Good placement: near the script panel or below the clip info header

Deliver: .shared/outputs/design/m14-spec.md with ASCII mockups + Tailwind class suggestions.

NEW MILESTONE M15: Performing Foundation Section — Full UI Design.

## CONTEXT
We are building a brand new section called "Performing Foundation" — completely separate from the movie clip practice section. It teaches fundamental communication skills through structured lessons. Think Udemy: courses → lessons → theory + examples + quiz.

Three courses:
1. Voice Mastery (🎙️) — vocal projection, tone, pacing, articulation, etc.
2. Verbal Communication (💬) — word choice, storytelling, assertiveness, etc.
3. Body Language Fundamentals (🤝) — posture, gestures, eye contact, etc.

Each lesson has THREE parts: Theory (rich text), Examples (YouTube clips), Quiz (MCQ).

## YOUR TASK
Design the complete Foundation section. Deliver to: .shared/outputs/design/m15-spec.md

### PAGE 1: Course Catalog — /foundation
- Hero header: "Performing Foundation" + tagline "Master the fundamentals of human communication"
- 3 course cards: icon, title, description, lesson count, user progress bar
- Each card links to /foundation/[courseSlug]
- Should feel distinct from the Library but consistent with seeneyu dark UI

### PAGE 2: Course Page — /foundation/[courseSlug]
- Course header: icon, title, full description, total lessons + estimated time
- Lesson list (ordered): lesson number, title, completion checkmark
- Locked/unlocked state: show whether lesson is completed
- Progress summary: "X of Y lessons complete"

### PAGE 3: Lesson Page — /foundation/[courseSlug]/[lessonSlug]
This is the most important page. Three distinct sections:

SECTION A: Theory
- Full-width rich text content (theoryHtml)
- Good typography: clear headings, readable body text, styled lists
- Estimated read time pill

SECTION B: Examples
- Sub-header: "See It In Action"
- YouTube embed cards: 2-3 per lesson
- Each card: embed + title + description of what to observe

SECTION C: Quiz
- Sub-header: "Test Your Understanding"
- Question cards (one at a time OR all visible — your design choice)
- MCQ options: 4 choices per question
- Submit button → reveal correct/wrong per question with explanation
- Score display: "3/4 correct — Lesson Complete!" 
- CTA: "Next Lesson →"

PROGRESS INDICATOR:
- Sidebar or top bar showing lesson position in course (e.g., "Lesson 3 of 10")
- Top: breadcrumb: Foundation > Voice Mastery > Vocal Projection

NAVIGATION:
- Bottom: ← Previous Lesson | Next Lesson →

### Design notes
- Dark UI consistent with seeneyu (#0d0d14 bg, amber accents)
- Distinct visual identity from the clip practice section (this is structured learning, not practice)
- Quiz: consider amber for correct, red for incorrect, gray for unselected
- Mobile-friendly layout

Deliver full spec with ASCII mockups + Tailwind class suggestions + component breakdown.
Backend Engineer is waiting on your spec before building.
